# Support ResourceLink (cross-space) resolution

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-07 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

Contentful introduced cross-space references (ResourceLinks) that use URN-based addressing rather than simple `{ type: 'Link', linkType, id }` objects. These links have the shape:
```json
{
  "sys": {
    "type": "ResourceLink",
    "linkType": "Contentful:Entry",
    "urn": "crn:contentful:::content:spaces/{spaceId}/environments/{envId}/entries/{entryId}"
  }
}
```

The existing resolution logic only handled `Link` type objects with flat ID lookups.

Commit `d856e24` (2022-12-07): "feat: resolve resource links"

## Decision

Extend the resolution algorithm to:
1. Detect `ResourceLink` objects via `sys.type === 'ResourceLink'`
2. Parse the URN to extract `spaceId`, `environmentId`, and `entryId`
3. Build composite entity map keys that include space and environment: `{spaceId}!{environmentId}!{type}!{id}`
4. Only resolve Contentful-provider links (prefix `Contentful:`); pass through links with unknown providers unchanged

A follow-up fix (`22a663e`, 2024-06-18) added explicit handling to ignore resource links with unknown providers when `removeUnresolved: true`, and another (`47e9b48`, 2024-07-30) ensured custom provider links are kept intact rather than removed.

## Consequences

- Cross-space entry references are now resolved automatically
- Entity map keys are composite (space + environment + type + id) enabling disambiguation
- Non-Contentful resource links are preserved as-is (forward-compatible with future providers)
- URN parsing uses a regex that assumes the Contentful CRN format
